titleStmt.title: Letter from P. J. Hannon to Nicolas Lamb, 21 September 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: P.J. Hannon

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from P. J. Hannon to Nicolas Lamb concerning the letting of a house and land near the Ballymacormick Graveyard.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Correspondence Longford 21st Sept 1916 Dear Mr Lamb In reference to yours of the 19th
inst addressed to the reps. of the late Mr F. M ford
regarding the letting of her house and land at
the Ballymacormick Graveyard to the keeper
of the graveyard for the convenience of the
public
public I am glad to say that at the families
request the Reps. of Mr. M ford are agreeable
to restore Mrs. Farrell to her former residence
the yearly rent to be Â£6. the rent to be
secured by a surety of some means. There is
an acre of land with the house so that the
rent is a fair one. It is right to say now
that whatever little repairs are to be done to
the premises Mrs. Farrell must do them at her
own expense. If Mrs. Farrell calls here
with her surety I will get up the agreement
and she can enter into possession at once. Yours Truly P.J. Hannon N Lamb Town Clerk.Longford
